description:
Test fixtures for lint rules. Each fixture asserts that running the full lint pass produces a specific set of rule hits. Rules with blocklist or regex are tested automatically; rules with natural-language `check` get a 'manual' marker.

Notes on expected hits:
- Short body fixtures expect body-too-short alongside the rule under test (intentional — real bodies need ≥100 words).
- 'clean' body fixtures are padded to ~110 words to avoid body-too-short noise.
- Title fixtures intentionally compose multiple violations to catch interaction bugs.

cases.name: clean-body-with-specific-ask
cases.input: I built a self-hosted cost tracker for API calls. One line change to your OpenAI client and every request shows up in a dashboard with the cost calculated per model. It runs as one Docker container, uses ClickHouse for storage, and the proxy adds under 5 milliseconds on the hot path. MIT licensed. The repo has the full schema documented if anyone wants to fork it. Curious which providers you would want supported next. Bedrock and Vertex are first in the queue, plus a few smaller ones if there is demand. Happy to dive into the cost calculation edge cases for fine-tuned models, cached prompts, and image inputs.
